note: Offline successor to the v1 live-LoC rights-images task (scored ~0.72). The catalog is shipped and frozen, so the answer is a deterministic set. The traps require reading the ITEM-level rights (not collection-level), checking the full image actually exists (not thumbnail), confirming the item is an image (not OCR text / sound), and deduplicating records that share an image.

requirements.item_level_rights_open: item_rights must be clearly open ('No known restrictions' or 'Public domain'); 'Restricted', 'undetermined', or 'advisory' rights do NOT qualify even if collection_rights looks open
requirements.full_image_available: image.full_image must be true (thumbnail-only does not count)
requirements.is_an_image: type must be a visual image (photograph); manuscript_text/sound_recording do NOT count
requirements.deduplicated: records sharing a dedup_group are the same image -> count once

acceptable_selection_rule: exactly 5 records, one from each of the 5 usable dedup groups: {g_migrant_mother(R01 or R02), g_brooklyn_bridge(R03 or R11), g_coney_island(R06), g_grand_central(R08), g_lincoln_memorial(R10)}. Picking BOTH R01 and R02 (or R03 and R11) is a dedup failure.

per_record.note: same image as R01; counts as the same item

per_record.detail: collection_rights is open but item_rights is 'Restricted' — item-level wins
type=manuscript_text (OCR'd text, not a visual image)
item_rights is advisory/undetermined, not a clear grant

scoring_anchors.item_vs_collection_rule: R05 is the key test: collection-level rights are open but item-level is restricted -> reject. An executor reading collection-level only wrongly accepts R05.
scoring_anchors.note: Full credit = exactly 5 records, one per usable dedup group, no trap included, no duplicate pair both selected, with each rejection citing the correct trap. Selecting both halves of a dedup pair, or including any trap, is the core failure.
